{{Infobox construct
| name = The Testament
| introduced = Of Sea and Shadow
}}

''See also: [[The Testament Timeline]]''

== Overview ==
{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-03-of-killers-and-kings|The Testament is a specialized vessel used by [[Navigators]], recognized as the fastest ship within the [[Guild]] due to its ability to travel in straight lines regardless of tides or wind. The ship features a dark green, nearly black wooden hull adorned with gold leaf lettering and sails that resemble stretched skin. While it contains various cabins for passengers and cargo, some areas are designed for minimal amenities, such as low-ceilinged supply spaces. The vessel is a [[Soulbound Vessel]], meaning it shares a mental bond with its captain, [[Calder Marten]], and can act with a sense of its own intent or panic. A defining characteristic of The Testament is the [[Lyathatan]], a massive [[Elder]] creature from [[Kelarac]] that is bound to the ship's underside by chains. This monster serves as the primary source of the ship's propulsion, pulling it through the ocean, though its cooperation can be difficult to maintain. The ship is commanded by [[Captain]] [[Calder Marten]] and has included crew members such as the cook, [[Urzaia Woodsman]]. Throughout its travels across the [[Aion]], the vessel has encountered various supernatural threats, including sentient ash clouds and intense magical conflicts.}}

== Of Sea and Shadow ==
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|The Testament is a dark green [[Awakened]] ship featuring green-veined sails made of membranous skin that resemble bat wings. [[Captain]] [[Calder Marten]] repaired the vessel using three hundred planks of wood, and he kept tools disguised as materials hidden in his carriage while working at the ship's scaffolding-covered work site. This fast [[Navigator vessel]], which serves as a [[Blackwatch]] ship, is designed to be pulled by an [[Elderspawn]] and contains cabins, cannons, and a hold filled with cargo. While [[Urzaia Woodsman]] serves as the official cook for the crew and prisoners, the ship also functions as a potential escape option that is expected to make landfall soon.}}

{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|[[The Testament]] is a ship with sails made of membranous green skin that resemble bat's wings.<ref name="ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow-c1">''Of Sea and Shadow'', chapter 1</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|[[The Testament]] is a ship that [[Calder Marten]] repaired using three hundred planks of wood.<ref name="ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow-c3">''Of Sea and Shadow'', chapter 3</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|[[The Testament]] is a ship.<ref name="ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow-c3" />}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|[[The Testament]] is a ship that can come to [[life]].<ref name="ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow-c5">''Of Sea and Shadow'', chapter 5</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|[[The Testament]] is the name of [[Calder's ship]].<ref name="ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow-c5" />}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|[[The Testament]] is a vessel or location with cabins.<ref name="ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow-c9">''Of Sea and Shadow'', chapter 9</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|[[Calder]] works on [[The Testament]].<ref name="ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow-c10">''Of Sea and Shadow'', chapter 10</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|[[The Testament]] is a ship that serves as an escape option.<ref name="ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow-c12">''Of Sea and Shadow'', chapter 12</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|[[The Testament]] has a work site.<ref name="ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow-c13">''Of Sea and Shadow'', chapter 13</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|[[The Testament]] is an [[Awakened]] ship.<ref name="ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow-c14">''Of Sea and Shadow'', chapter 14</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|[[The Testament]] is the fastest ship in the [[Guild]].<ref name="ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow-c14" />}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|[[The Testament]] is an object whose shadow looms over the characters.<ref name="ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow-c16">''Of Sea and Shadow'', chapter 16</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|[[The Testament]] is a vessel with a hold containing barrels, crates, and bundles of cargo.<ref name="ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow-c17">''Of Sea and Shadow'', chapter 17</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|[[The Testament]] is a ship intended to be pulled by an [[Elderspawn]].<ref name="ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow-c19">''Of Sea and Shadow'', chapter 19</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|[[The Testament]] is almost finished.<ref name="ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow-c19" />}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|[[Calder]] has tools disguised as materials from [[The Testament]] hidden in his carriage.<ref name="ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow-c19" />}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|[[Urzaia Woodsman]] is the official cook aboard the [[Navigator vessel]] known as [[The Testament]].<ref name="ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow-c20">''Of Sea and Shadow'', chapter 20</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|[[Calder]] is the captain of [[The Testament]].<ref name="ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow-c20" />}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|[[Jerri]] failed to persuade [[Calder]] to steal [[The Testament]].<ref name="ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow-c21">''Of Sea and Shadow'', chapter 21</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|[[The Testament]] is expected to make landfall soon.<ref name="ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow-c23">''Of Sea and Shadow'', chapter 23</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|[[The Testament]] is a ship with green-veined sails.<ref name="ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow-c23" />}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|[[The Testament]] is an object the [[Blackwatch]] aims to use to summon something capable of dragging a ship through the ocean.<ref name="ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow-c24">''Of Sea and Shadow'', chapter 24</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|[[The Testament]] is a dark green ship.<ref name="ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow-c24" />}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|[[The Testament]] is a ship that has wooden scaffolding surrounding it.<ref name="ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow-c24" />}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|[[The Testament]]'s sails are made of greenish skin with pulsing veins.<ref name="ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow-c24" />}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|[[The Testament]] is a [[Blackwatch]] ship.<ref name="ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow-c26">''Of Sea and Shadow'', chapter 26</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|[[The Testament]] is a destination or entity mentioned by [[Calder]].<ref name="ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow-c27">''Of Sea and Shadow'', chapter 27</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|[[The Testament]] is a structure equipped with cannons.<ref name="ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow-c27" />}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|[[The Testament]] is a vessel that carries crewmen and prisoners.<ref name="ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow-c27" />}}

== Of Shadow and Sea ==
{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea|[[The Testament]] is a place where [[Shera]] spends her first night.<ref name="ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea-c17">''Of Shadow and Sea'', chapter 17</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea|[[The Testament]] is a vessel that docks at the docks.<ref name="ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea-c19">''Of Shadow and Sea'', chapter 19</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea|[[The Testament]] is a location that [[Gardeners]] could depopulate.<ref name="ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea-c27">''Of Shadow and Sea'', chapter 27</ref>}}

== Of Dawn and Darkness ==
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|The Testament is a sailing vessel owned by [[Calder Marten]] that [[Emperor (Elder Empire)|the Emperor]] has invested thousands of [[goldmarks]] into. After [[Calder]] unsuccessfully attempted to tip the object, the group eventually took possession of the ship, which was stuffed full of salvaged cargo. Thirteen people used the vessel to leave [[Silverreach]], including [[Petal]], though its crew was later taken prisoner by the [[Sleepless]]. [[Calder]] uses [[Reading]] to keep the ship held together, intending to use it as a means of flight that can even sneak an [[Inquisitor]] aboard. This constantly moving boat features a railing and remains a construction that [[Jorin]] possesses the power to sink.}}

{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|[[The Testament]] is an object [[Calder]] tried to tip.<ref name="ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness-c2">''Of Dawn and Darkness'', chapter 2</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|The group has taken [[The Testament]].<ref name="ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness-c3">''Of Dawn and Darkness'', chapter 3</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|[[The Testament]] is a ship that is currently stuffed to the brim with salvaged cargo.<ref name="ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness-c4">''Of Dawn and Darkness'', chapter 4</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|[[The Testament]] is [[Calder Marten]]'s ship.<ref name="ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness-c5">''Of Dawn and Darkness'', chapter 5</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|[[The Testament]] is an artifact mentioned by [[Cheska]].<ref name="ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness-c6">''Of Dawn and Darkness'', chapter 6</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|[[Calder]] uses [[Reading]] to hold [[The Testament]] together.<ref name="ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness-c6" />}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|[[Calder]]'s clothes were carried over from [[The Testament]].<ref name="ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness-c8">''Of Dawn and Darkness'', chapter 8</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|[[The Testament]] is a ship [[Calder]] intends to use to flee.<ref name="ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness-c8" />}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|[[The Testament]] is a boat that never stops moving.<ref name="ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness-c10">''Of Dawn and Darkness'', chapter 10</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|[[The Testament]] is a ship that an [[Inquisitor]] could be sneaked into.<ref name="ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness-c11">''Of Dawn and Darkness'', chapter 11</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|[[The Testament]] is a ship whose crew was taken prisoner by the [[Sleepless]].<ref name="ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness-c13">''Of Dawn and Darkness'', chapter 13</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|[[The Testament]] is a ship that thirteen people used to leave [[Silverreach]].<ref name="ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness-c13" />}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|[[Petal]] is aboard [[The Testament]].<ref name="ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness-c19">''Of Dawn and Darkness'', chapter 19</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|[[The Testament]] is a construction that [[Emperor (Elder Empire)|the Emperor]] has invested thousands of [[goldmarks]] into.<ref name="ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness-c19" />}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|[[The Testament]] is a vessel that [[Jorin]] could sink.<ref name="ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness-c20">''Of Dawn and Darkness'', chapter 20</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|[[The Testament]] has a railing.<ref name="ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness-c22">''Of Dawn and Darkness'', chapter 22</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|[[The Testament]] is a force that can be capsized like a crashing wave.<ref name="ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness-c23">''Of Dawn and Darkness'', chapter 23</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|[[The Testament]] is a vessel with sails.<ref name="ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness-c24">''Of Dawn and Darkness'', chapter 24</ref>}}

== Of Darkness and Dawn ==
{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn|[[The Testament]] has a crew of at least three members.<ref name="ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn-c15">''Of Darkness and Dawn'', chapter 15</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn|[[Shera]] faced the entire crew of [[The Testament]].<ref name="ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn-c17">''Of Darkness and Dawn'', chapter 17</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn|[[The Testament]] is a ship leading the [[Navigators]].<ref name="ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn-c22">''Of Darkness and Dawn'', chapter 22</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn|[[The Testament]] has green-veined sails and chains attached to an [[Elder]] monstrosity.<ref name="ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn-c22" />}}

== Of Kings and Killers ==
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|The Testament is a vessel that remains prohibited for any [[Soulbound]] to carry into a meeting room. While [[Foster]] maintains a cabin aboard the ship, he attempts to bring the vessel close to shore where waves lap against its half-wrecked hull. After being picked up by the ship in the shallow Aion, a group plans to board the craft near the harbor. The arrival of the ship is viewed as a godsend, especially as it emits a pure light from its deck and responds to the presence of [[Calder]]. Although the ship contains only a crew and no passengers, it serves as a location where people can look up toward its structure.}}

{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|[[The Testament]] is an item that a [[Soulbound]] cannot bring into a meeting room.<ref name="ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers-c4">''Of Kings and Killers'', chapter 4</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|[[Calder]] ran back to [[The Testament]].<ref name="ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers-c5">''Of Kings and Killers'', chapter 5</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|[[Calder]] wears clothes similar to what he would wear aboard [[The Testament]].<ref name="ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers-c6">''Of Kings and Killers'', chapter 6</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|[[The Testament]] is a ship that [[Foster]] is attempting to bring close to shore.<ref name="ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers-c12">''Of Kings and Killers'', chapter 12</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|[[The Testament]] is a vessel with waves lapping against it.<ref name="ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers-c14">''Of Kings and Killers'', chapter 14</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|[[The Testament]] is a ship that [[Dalton Foster]] has a cabin on.<ref name="ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers-c17">''Of Kings and Killers'', chapter 17</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|[[The Testament]] is a vessel that picks up [[Foster]] in the shallow Aion.<ref name="ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers-c18">''Of Kings and Killers'', chapter 18</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|The group plans to get aboard [[The Testament]].<ref name="ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers-c19">''Of Kings and Killers'', chapter 19</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|[[The Testament]] is a ship that responds to [[Calder]]'s presence.<ref name="ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers-c20">''Of Kings and Killers'', chapter 20</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|[[The Testament]] is a structure or location near the harbor.<ref name="ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers-c22">''Of Kings and Killers'', chapter 22</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|[[The Testament]] is a location [[Calder]] hurries back to.<ref name="ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers-c22" />}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|[[The Testament]] is a ship that emits a pure light from its deck.<ref name="ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers-c22" />}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|The arrival of [[The Testament]] was considered a godsend.<ref name="ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers-c23">''Of Kings and Killers'', chapter 23</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|[[The Testament]] is a ship in the fleet that has no passengers, only crew.<ref name="ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers-c23" />}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|[[The Testament]] is a location where people can look up.<ref name="ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers-c23" />}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|[[The Testament]] is a ship that was half a wreck.<ref name="ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers-c25">''Of Kings and Killers'', chapter 25</ref>}}

== Of Killers and Kings ==
{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-03-of-killers-and-kings|[[Calder Marten]] might have worn clothes appropriate for a ship's deck aboard [[The Testament]].<ref name="ee-shadow-03-of-killers-and-kings-c24">''Of Killers and Kings'', chapter 24</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-03-of-killers-and-kings|[[The Testament]] is tied to a monster.<ref name="ee-shadow-03-of-killers-and-kings-c25">''Of Killers and Kings'', chapter 25</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-03-of-killers-and-kings|[[Shera]] and [[Calder]] first met on [[The Testament]].<ref name="ee-shadow-03-of-killers-and-kings-c25" />}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-03-of-killers-and-kings|[[The Testament]] is being pulled forward by an [[Elder]] creature below the ocean's surface.<ref name="ee-shadow-03-of-killers-and-kings-c25" />}}

== Notable quotes ==
* {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|“Over long distances, The Testament is the fastest ship in the Guild.<ref name="ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow-c14" />}}
* {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|“We have taken The Testament,” Jerri responded.<ref name="ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness-c3" />}}

== Notes ==
<references />

{{Elder Empire nav}}

[[Category:Constructs]]
[[Category:Of Sea and Shadow]]
[[Category:Of Dawn and Darkness]]
[[Category:Of Kings and Killers]]
[[Category:Of Shadow and Sea]]
[[Category:Of Darkness and Dawn]]
[[Category:Of Killers and Kings]]
[[Category:Elder Empire]]
